shower.thought: We spend the first half of our lives learning who we are, and the second half unlearning it.
If a tree falls in the forest and no one's around, it still makes a sound—but we'll never know if it's the same sound we hear when we're listening.
You've probably had the exact same thought as someone else at the exact same moment, and you'll never know it happened.
The moment you become aware of your own mortality is the moment you stop being completely alive.
Your shadow has spent your entire life watching you from behind, and you've never once turned around fast enough to catch it doing anything.
Most of your life is spent preparing for moments you'll forget about immediately after they happen.
We're all performing for an audience of people too busy performing to actually watch us.
The people you hate most are usually mirrors you haven't figured out yet.
You can't remember 99% of your life, but 100% of it shaped who you are right now.
Forgetting someone's name seconds after they tell you might be the first honest thing you do in a conversation.
Time only moves in one direction, but your regrets seem to travel in all of them at once.
If you've ever felt like nobody understands you, that's because nobody has access to the inside of your head—which is probably lonely enough without the confirmation.
Every time you learn something new, you're basically admitting you were wrong about something you didn't even know you were wrong about.
The most dangerous people aren't the ones who are certain they're right—they're the ones who just realized they might be wrong.
You're living in the past because light takes time to reach your eyes, so technically, you've never seen anything in real-time.
The longer you know someone, the less you actually see them and the more you see who you've decided they are.
Boredom might just be your brain's way of telling you it's about to grow.
Everyone's got a version of themselves they show the world and a version they show nobody, but the second version is the most honest.
You can't actually prove that other people experience colors the same way you do, so your favorite color might be completely different from everyone else's favorite color.
We're all just trying to leave proof that we were here, but we'll probably erase most of it by accident before we go.

motivational.thought:
The obstacle is not blocking your path—it is the path.
— Unknown

Don't wait for the perfect moment. Take the moment and make it perfect.
— Unknown

Progress whispers while perfection screams. Listen to progress.
— Unknown

You are not behind. You are not lost. You are exactly where you need to be to become who you're meant to be.
— Unknown

The most dangerous phrase is "I'll start tomorrow." Today is the only day you have control over.
— Unknown

Discipline is choosing what you want most over what you want now.
— Unknown

Your potential is infinite. Your excuses are finite. Choose accordingly.
— Unknown

The difference between a dream and a goal is a deadline and a commitment.
— Unknown

Small daily improvements are the antidote to envy, resentment, and disappointment.
— Unknown

You don't rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems.
— Unknown

The person you become is worth more than the comfort you give up.
— Unknown

Every moment is a choice. Choose growth.
— Unknown

Your future self is watching you right now. Make them proud.
— Unknown

Courage is not the absence of fear. It is the decision that something else matters more.
— Unknown

The best revenge is not bitterness. It is becoming who you always had the potential to be.
— Unknown

What separates the extraordinary from the ordinary is not talent—it's consistency.
— Unknown

You don't need permission to start. You need only the willingness to fail in front of others.
— Unknown

Pressure either breaks you or builds you. The difference is your perspective.
— Unknown

The only time you fail is when you stop trying to succeed.
— Unknown

Success is a series of small wins compounded over time. Start winning today.
— Unknown

darkjoke.thought:
My therapist asked if I ever feel like people don't listen to me.
I don't think she heard the question.

I told my friend he was being paranoid about his girlfriend cheating.
He said, "You're right, thanks for the support."
I said, "That's what I'm here for," and left through her bedroom window.

The doctor told me I have six months to live.
I said, "I can't pay the bill."
He said, "Then you have two weeks."

My dad said he wanted to be cremated when he died.
I said, "Why wait?"

I tried to tell my boss my mental health was suffering.
He said, "That's not a company problem."
I said, "It will be on Monday."

I asked my girlfriend if she'd ever cheat on me.
She said, "Would you even notice?"
That's when I realized I should stop following her around.

My therapist told me I'm in denial about my drinking problem.
I said, "I don't have a drinking problem—you do."
She hadn't said anything about drinking.

I told my friend his eulogy was beautiful.
He said, "Thanks, I've been workshopping it for years."

My wife said I never listen to her.
At least that's what I think she said—I wasn't paying attention.

I went to a wedding recently.
Two people I loved got married.
Now I only love one.

My doctor said I needed to cut back on sodium.
I said, "Will that help my blood pressure?"
He said, "No, but it'll stop the slugs from eating you in the garden."

I asked my friend why he doesn't talk about his depression anymore.
He said, "I'm doing great now, actually."
We found his note three days later.

My mom said she'd never forgive me for what I did.
I said, "I haven't told you yet."
She said, "Exactly."

I told my coworker his hair was thinning.
He said, "Should I be worried?"
I said, "Not yet, but your wife should be."

My therapist said I have abandonment issues.
So I fired her.

I asked my friend how his new antidepressants were working.
He said, "Amazing, I haven't felt like killing myself in weeks."
I said, "That's great!"
He said, "Yeah, now I just feel like killing other people."

My girlfriend left me because I'm too negative.
I really saw that coming.

I told my dad jokes at his surprise birthday party.
He said, "These are terrible."
I said, "Yeah, you passed that gene to me."

My friend said he was cutting people out of his life.
I said, "Good for you."
He said, "You're first."
I said, "Take a number."

The priest asked if anyone objected to the marriage.
I raised my hand.
Everyone looked at me confused.
Apparently "I do" means something different.
